title: Measuring Inference Performance
concept: Key metrics: Time to First Token (TTFT, latency) and Tokens per Second (throughput, cost efficiency).

exercises.prompt: What does 'inference' mean in the context of AI models?

options.text: Running a trained model to get answers
Training a model from scratch with data
Deleting outdated data from the model

exercises.explain: Inference is the phase where a trained AI model actually processes your input prompt and generates a response.

cards.title: Is Your AI Fast?
cards.body: Have you ever noticed how some AI bots start replying instantly, while others make you wait before spitting out text at lightning speed? Let's learn how we measure this speed!

cards.title: TTFT vs. Throughput
cards.body: Inference performance is measured in two main ways: Time to First Token (TTFT), which is the delay before the AI starts typing its first word, and Throughput (Tokens per Second), which is how quickly it streams the rest of the text.

cards.title: The Sandwich Shop
cards.body: Imagine ordering a custom sandwich at a deli. Time to First Token (TTFT) is how quickly the cashier hands you the first slice of bread so you know they are working on it. Tokens per Second (throughput) is how fast they assemble and wrap the rest of the sandwich. You want both a quick start and a fast finish!

cards.title: Real-World Speed
cards.body: If an AI translator takes 2 full seconds to think before typing but then prints 100 words per second, it has a high TTFT (slow start) but high throughput (fast speed). If it starts immediately but only types 2 words per second, it has low TTFT (fast start) but low throughput.

cards.title: The Speed Myth
cards.body: People often think 'fast AI' just means one thing. But an AI can be slow to start (high TTFT) yet super fast once it gets going (high throughput), or vice versa. We must look at both metrics!

cards.title: Performance Metrics Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare the two key speed metrics we use to evaluate AI models in production.

left.text: Measures responsiveness. How long the user waits before the AI starts printing the first word.

right.text: Measures delivery speed and efficiency. How many words the AI outputs per second once it starts.

exercises.prompt: What does the performance metric acronym TTFT stand for?
exercises.explain: TTFT stands for Time to First Token. It measures the pause between when you press send and when the AI prints its very first character.

exercises.prompt: A higher 'Tokens per Second' rate means the AI is generating text faster.
exercises.explain: Tokens per Second (throughput) measures how much text is generated per second. A higher number means the AI is faster and more cost-efficient.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about AI performance metrics.
exercises.sentence: While TTFT measures how fast the AI starts, Throughput measures how many tokens the AI can produce every second.
exercises.explain: Throughput measures the total volume of words (tokens) generated per second, indicating the system's efficiency.

exercises.prompt: A chatbot starts typing the very millisecond you send a message, but it only outputs one word every five seconds. Which profile fits this chatbot?
exercises.explain: The chatbot starts typing immediately, which means it has a low (fast) Time to First Token (TTFT). However, it types very slowly after that, meaning it has low throughput.

exercises.prompt: You are building a real-time chatbot for a customer support app. Which performance metric combination will make your bot feel the most responsive and run the most efficiently?

options.text: Low TTFT (fast first token) and High Throughput (many tokens per second)
High TTFT (slow first token) and Low Throughput (few tokens per second)
Low TTFT (fast first token) and Low Throughput (few tokens per second)
High TTFT (slow first token) and High Throughput (many tokens per second)

exercises.explain: Lower TTFT means users see the model start replying immediately, which makes it feel responsive. Higher throughput means the model can handle more words per second, making it fast and cost-effective overall.
